title: Introduction to Core Modules & The fs Module (Reading)
description: Learn what core modules are and how to read files with `fs`.

slides.title: What are Core Modules? 📦
slides.content:
# Node.js Core Modules

Node.js comes with a set of built-in modules that you can use without installing any external packages. These are called **core modules**.

Examples:
- `fs` – file system
- `path` – file paths
- `os` – operating system
- `http` – HTTP server/client
- `crypto` – cryptography

To use a core module, you simply `require` it:
```javascript
const fs = require('fs');
```

quizData.question: What does `fs.readFile` return?

options.text: A Buffer if no encoding provided

quizData.explanation: Without encoding, `data` is a Buffer. With encoding like 'utf8', it returns a string.
